name: ion-night-overlay-nyc
description: Create a viewer centered on the northeastern United States and add Cesium ion Earth at Night imagery as a semi-transparent overlay. Tests the async factory path and runtime layer display tuning.
prompt: Create a CesiumJS globe centered on New York City and the surrounding northeastern United States. Keep the default world imagery as the base layer, then add Cesium ion Earth at Night as a semi-transparent overlay so city lights are visible over the region. Set the overlay alpha below 1.0 and slightly increase its brightness. Use a camera height high enough to show the New York to Boston corridor.
expected_behaviors: Uses IonImageryProvider.fromAssetId with the Earth at Night imagery asset
Adds the imagery layer to viewer.imageryLayers
Sets alpha below 1.0 on the overlay layer
Sets brightness above 1.0 on the overlay layer
Positions the camera over the northeastern United States
visual_expectations: A globe view of the northeastern United States with visible night lights over the New York City region and nearby urban corridor. The overlay should not completely hide the underlying imagery.

screenshots.description: Night overlay visible over NYC corridor
